threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when you see a bus driver chatting away with the regulars while you’re waiting for the next delayed tube.
Last week on the 133, a guy serenaded the entire bus with Sea Shanties. No one clapped, but everyone couldn't stop smiling.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was truly a Neuköllner when I started debating the nuances of kebab toppings with my neighbors at midnight.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can get a traditional Turkish breakfast with gözleme at 3 AM here—nothing like it anywhere else. It’s a vibe that just hits different.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local if they can name at least three different taco spots and know which days to avoid the BART delays. It’s an art.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggle while waiting for the 5. He effortlessly kept three balls in the air, just like Muni keeps dodging punctuality.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all of Gràcia’s plazas are just for photos; locals actually gather for cheap wine and gossip in between the tapas bars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll spot them near Plaça del Sol, staring bewildered at the bus maps, fumbling with their Google Translate in a paella restaurant.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'cabbie' can get you anywhere faster than the bus. Enjoy those delays on the 38, mate.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and a guy serenaded the whole ride with a ukulele—got off at Stockwell, folks were still smiling.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy casually juggling in front of the bakery while a dog inspected the pastries. Just another morning in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you see an entire park full of amateur DJs blasting techno at 3 PM on a Tuesday. It's a vibe no other city matches.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by how they roll their eyes at the secondhand smoke wafting from someone's incense stick at Dolores Park.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy selling homemade paletas on the 31 bus yesterday. Whole ride turned into a mini street fair—classic San Francisco vibe, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss how quiet Gràcia's streets get after 10 PM; it’s like a village that holds its breath, but the bars stay lively as ever.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the number of times they look perplexed at metro signs; the locals just glide in and out, no second glances.
